session.name: MPI-148 ComfyUI v0.27 app+Pod bump - shipped; live-verify surfaced OOM + download bugs

goal.original: Bump ComfyUI core v0.26.0 -> v0.27.0 (app + Pod), build + publish the Pod images, then live-verify on a GPU Pod.
goal.summary: MPI-148 code + images SHIPPED and public (cpu/cu128/cu124 all v0.11.0, ComfyUI v0.27); cpu smoke + CPU-Pod live-boot verified. GPU live gen-verify FAILED with an LTX OOM that is a real separate issue (GGUF dequant spike, carded MPI-185). Wan 5B + PiD generated OK on the same Pod, so the v0.27 bump itself is sound. Three new bug cards opened during verify (MPI-183 Builder bump deferred, MPI-184 concurrent-install wrapper bugs, MPI-185 LTX OOM + perf + gallery blob 404s).

task_card.title: Bump ComfyUI core v0.26.0 -> v0.27.0 (app + Pod)

MPI-185 created (LTX OOM GGUF-dequant + TAESD-None + perf + gallery blob-404)

next_action.description: Pick up MPI-185 (LTX i2v OOM on 24GB cloud 4090). Root cause is pinned: the OOM is in ComfyUI-GGUF/dequant.py:62 dequantize_blocks_BF16 (int16->int32->float32 upcast spike) at sample time - the Q8 GGUF transformer already ~22GB partial-loaded under lowvram, and the dequant's +576MB tips past 24GB. Read MPI-185's description + files.json, then decide: (a) is it res/tier-specific (retry lower LTX tier, log the exact i2v_ms resolution), (b) v0.26-vs-v0.27 regression test, (c) use the BF16 (non-GGUF) transformer or a smaller quant on the 24GB/broad tier, or (d) --reserve-vram/--disable-dynamic-vram to leave dequant headroom. Once LTX i2v gens clean on v0.27, move MPI-148 to done.

key_decisions: MPI-148 rescoped from 'Builder-only' to app+Pod bump; Builder split to MPI-183 (user deferred).
Card missed frontend pins - added comfyui-frontend-package 1.45.20 + workflow-templates 0.11.1.
node_lock core is Pod-build input + parity, NOT the app runtime engine (engine/ is gitignored, separate manual portable swap).
Pod image versioned v0.11.0 (minor bump for engine floor move); wrapper unchanged 0.2.23.
Weight-prebake moved HF->R2 (MPI-148 fix) because HF Xet CDN 403s broke the build; weights live at G:/CubricModels + R2.
OOM is a SEPARATE issue from the v0.27 bump - Wan 5B + PiD gen'd fine on the same Pod, so v0.27 itself is sound.
constraints: Live Pod ops (create/deploy/stop) are USER-only; agent guides, user clicks.
Push is user-authorized; mpi-ci was pushed (authorized), app RunPod branch commits are local-only.
GGUF dequant BF16 = int16->int32->float32 = ~2x transient VRAM spike; the balanced LTX Pod path uses Q8 GGUF (MPI-168).
aimdo/dynamic-vram is active on the Pod ('loaded partially ... lowvram patches: 0').
Verify R2 URLs with a SINGLE curl -sI (looped curl -o lies HTTP 000 - schannel).
docker rmi of pre-existing tags is classifier-blocked; don't force-delete images the user didn't name.

resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/5f82466f-de7e-46d8-a3e5-cace5a8a9a3f.json and use mpi-continue to resume. Context: MPI-148 (ComfyUI v0.26->v0.27 app+Pod bump) is SHIPPED - all three v0.11.0 Pod images (cpu/cu128/cu124) built + public, cpu smoke + CPU-Pod live-boot verified, mpi-ci pushed (bc649a7, 1911aef), app pins committed local (54a62ce). During GPU live gen-verify, Wan 5B + PiD generated fine on v0.27 but LTX-2.3 i2v OOM'd on a 24GB cloud 4090. That OOM is carded as MPI-185 (HIGH): root cause pinned to ComfyUI-GGUF/dequant.py:62 dequantize_blocks_BF16 - the Q8 GGUF transformer is ~22GB partial-loaded under lowvram and the int16->int32->float32 dequant spike (+576MB) tips past the 23.64GiB limit. Start by reading MPI-185's task.json + files.json, then investigate: is it LTX tier/resolution specific, a v0.26-vs-v0.27 regression, or does the 24GB/broad tier need the BF16 (non-GGUF) transformer or --reserve-vram headroom? Two more bugs were carded from this session: MPI-184 (concurrent installs starve the CPU-pod wrapper: SSE 'bad-response' stall + false 'archive produced no folder') and MPI-183 (Builder image v0.27 bump, deferred). Once LTX i2v gens clean on v0.27, move MPI-148 to done. Live Pod ops are user-only; app-branch commits stay local until the user authorizes a push.
